
Two arXiv papers from the RSS scout stand out as strong candidates. 'HyEvo: Self-Evolving Hybrid Agentic Workflows for Efficient Reasoning' directly addresses agentic workflow architecture with self-evolution and hybrid design. 'Utility-Guided Agent Orchestration for Efficient LLM Tool Use' covers orchestration and tool-use design with empirical grounding. The Grok social signals (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) are plausible but lack verified recency/content confirmation for this window. The two arXiv papers are fresh, on-topic, and meet ≥3 criteria each.

## Suggestions

- [HyEvo: Self-Evolving Hybrid Agentic Workflows for Efficient Reasoning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.19639) — Proposes a self-evolving hybrid agentic workflow architecture that dynamically adapts workflow structure and agent composition for efficient multi-step reasoning tasks.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Tool-use and execution design across agent lifecycle stages (planning, execution, feedback, recovery), Iterative refinement practices (evaluation loops, human-in-the-loop, observability), Empirical or practical grounding (case studies, benchmarks, production deployment evidence)

- [Utility-Guided Agent Orchestration for Efficient LLM Tool Use](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.19896) — Presents a utility-guided orchestration framework for selecting and sequencing LLM tool calls efficiently, with empirical evaluation across multi-step agentic tasks.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Tool-use and execution design across agent lifecycle stages (planning, execution, feedback, recovery), Empirical or practical grounding (case studies, benchmarks, production deployment evidence)
